properties.name: Status Panel
properties.description: The panel now displays a detailed log overlay from the data chip. Among the rows of green text, one entry in BAY C-4 stands out in bright amber: OCCUPANT REVIVED - 242 YEARS AGO - STATUS: DEPARTED.
properties.takeRefusal: The panel is bolted to the wall.
properties.secret: Tapping a specific sequence on the panel pages through to other bays’ status, revealing one bay with an anomalous reading that suggests its occupant revived long ago.

scenery.description: The status 'DEPARTED' is rendered in a blocky, high-contrast font that pulses slowly on the panel. Unlike the standard logs, this entry lacks a subsequent 'RE-ENTRY' or 'DECEASED' flag, suggesting the individual simply walked out and never looked back.
scenery.rejection: The text is just a digital record; you cannot follow someone who left two centuries ago.

properties.name: Corridor C
properties.description: A broad corridor with gently curved walls of brushed metal. Colored guide strips run along the floor — green toward the bow, red toward the stern. Recessed lighting panels overhead flicker intermittently, some dead entirely. A faded mural on one wall depicts people planting trees under a stylized sun. Below it, someone has scratched smaller figures in a different style.
properties.aiPrompt: The first step into the wider ship. The player should start noticing that this place has layers — the mural and the scratched figures hint that different people lived here at different times. This corridor connects the safe starting area to the rest of the deck. Objects here should be transitional: signage, wall markings, small hints of the ship's history.
properties.secret: The scratched figures below the mural are arranged in a specific pattern — they are actually a map of the deck drawn by a later generation of inhabitants.

scenery.description: These primitive, thin-limbed stick figures are etched deeply into the metal, forming a sprawling web of intersecting lines and dots. They don't seem to be dancing; instead, each figure stands at a specific junction, their outstretched arms pointing toward peculiar geometric shapes.
scenery.rejection: The etchings are cold and permanent, scarred into the wall by a very sharp tool.
